page: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by custom AWS Graviton3 processors utilizing the ARM64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU is based on the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture, equipped with 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, has a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps, and does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory subsystem capabilities. The server achieves top-tier memory performance, as evidenced by its PassMark memory score and high memory bandwidth metrics, reaching 4652.22 GB/sec cached read and 252.61 GB/sec uncached read. Multi-core processing is highly efficient, with strong ratings in stress-ng multi-core tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation tasks. Single-core performance and cryptographic operations fall within the average range. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput are also highly rated, though LLM text generation speeds for smaller models represent a relative performance bottleneck.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based Graviton architecture delivers high resource density and strong multi-core throughput relative to its power and resource footprint. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable, dedicated core performance without resource contention. This profile makes the m7g.12xlarge highly suitable for multi-threaded workloads, including database hosting, Redis caching layers, static web serving, software compilation pipelines, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by custom AWS Graviton3 processors built on the ARM64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz, with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks highlight top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core processing capabilities, particularly in software compilation, database operations, and Redis caching. Single-core performance and cryptographic tasks remain average. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable performance, making the m7g.12xlarge highly efficient for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 48 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated cores and high-bandwidth memory for predictable multi-threaded application performance.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton3 ARM64 physical cores at 2.6 GHz.
Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory and 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core processing performance.
Operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with no local storage or GPU accelerators.
Best suited for database hosting, Redis caching, software compilation, and web serving.
